Guideline: Use the App Router for new projects
Description: App Router provides improved performance and features over Pages Router
Do: Use app/ directory for new Next.js 13+ projects
Don't: Start new projects with Pages Router

Description: Each folder in app/ represents a route segment
Do: Use folders for URL segments with page.tsx inside
Don't: Create routes with individual files

Description: Create dynamic segments using [param] folder names
Do: Use [id] for single params [...slug] for catch-all
Don't: Hardcode all possible routes

Description: Group routes without affecting URL structure
Do: Use (marketing) (shop) for logical grouping
Don't: Create unnecessary URL nesting

Guideline: Use parallel routes for complex layouts
Description: Render multiple pages in same layout simultaneously
Do: Use @modal @sidebar for parallel rendering
Don't: Load everything in single page component

Description: Server Components reduce client bundle size and improve performance
Do: Keep components server-side unless client features needed
Don't: Add use client to every component

Guideline: Add use client only when necessary
Description: Client components are needed for hooks events and browser APIs
Do: Add use client for useState useEffect onClick
Don't: Add use client for static display components
Code Good: 'use client'; function Counter() { const [count, setCount] = useState(0) }

Guideline: Push client boundaries down the tree
Description: Keep server components as parents passing data to client children
Do: Extract interactive parts into small client components
Don't: Make entire page tree client components

Description: Create loading UI that shows while route segments load
Do: Add loading.tsx next to page.tsx for automatic loading UI
Don't: Handle all loading states manually in components

Description: Create error handling UI for route segments
Do: Add error.tsx with reset function for recovery
Don't: Let errors crash the application

Description: Fetch data directly in async Server Components
Do: Use async/await in server components
Don't: Fetch all data on client with useEffect
Code Good: export default async function Page() { const data = await fetch() }

Guideline: Use React cache for request deduplication
Description: Deduplicate fetch requests across components
Do: Wrap fetch functions with React cache
Don't: Fetch same data in multiple components
Code Bad: // Same fetch in Header Footer Sidebar

Description: Control caching behavior with fetch options
Do: Use revalidate and cache options explicitly
Don't: Rely on default caching for all requests

Description: Purge cache when data changes
Do: Call revalidatePath after mutations
Don't: Let stale data persist after updates

Description: Stream UI progressively as data loads
Do: Wrap slow components in Suspense boundaries
Don't: Wait for all data before rendering anything

Description: Next.js Image component provides automatic optimization
Do: Import Image from next/image
Don't: Use HTML img tag directly

Description: Prevent layout shift with explicit dimensions
Do: Set width and height or use fill prop
Don't: Omit dimensions causing layout shift

Description: Fill parent container with object-fit styling
Do: Use fill with relative positioned parent
Don't: Use fixed width/height for all images

Description: Allow external images from specific domains
Do: Add domains or remotePatterns to next.config.js
Don't: Leave remote images unconfigured causing errors

Description: Mark above-fold images as priority for faster loading
Do: Add priority to hero images and logos
Don't: Let critical images lazy load

Description: Automatic font optimization and zero layout shift
Do: Import fonts from next/font/google or local
Don't: Load fonts with link or @import

Description: Use CSS variables for flexible font application
Do: Set font as CSS variable in layout
Don't: Apply font class to every element

Description: Include only needed character subsets
Do: Specify subset option in font config
Don't: Load entire font including unused characters

Description: Use metadata export for static page metadata
Do: Export const metadata from page.tsx
Don't: Add meta tags manually in head

Description: Generate metadata based on route params or fetched data
Do: Export async generateMetadata function
Don't: Hardcode metadata for dynamic pages
Code Bad: export const metadata = { title: 'Product' } // not dynamic

Description: Set og:image for social media previews
Do: Add openGraph.images to metadata
Don't: Omit Open Graph tags from pages

Description: Configure app icons in metadata
Do: Set icons property in root layout metadata
Don't: Add favicon.ico manually to public

Guideline: Use Route Handlers for API endpoints
Description: Create API routes with route.ts in app directory
Do: Export HTTP method functions from route.ts
Don't: Create API pages in pages/api
Code Bad: export async function GET(request) { return Response.json({}) }
Severity: export default function handler(req res) {} // Pages Router

Description: Export separate functions for GET POST etc
Do: Export GET POST PUT DELETE as needed
Don't: Handle all methods in single function
Code Good: export async function GET() {} export async function POST() {}

Description: Server Actions simplify form submissions and data mutations
Do: Use use server for form actions
Don't: Create API routes for all mutations
Code Bad: // API route + fetch for every form

Description: Always validate and sanitize user input
Do: Use zod or similar for input validation
Don't: Trust client-side validation only

Guideline: Use middleware for auth and redirects
Description: Middleware runs before requests are completed
Do: Add middleware.ts to project root
Don't: Handle auth in every page component

Description: Limit middleware to specific paths
Do: Use matcher config to target paths
Don't: Let middleware run on all requests including static assets
Code Bad: // No matcher - runs on every request

Description: Middleware runs on every matched request
Do: Only do essential checks in middleware
Don't: Perform heavy operations in middleware

Guideline: Use NEXT_PUBLIC_ prefix for client variables
Description: Only NEXT_PUBLIC_ vars are exposed to browser
Do: Prefix client-needed vars with NEXT_PUBLIC_
Don't: Expose sensitive vars to client

Description: Never expose API keys or secrets to client
Do: Access secrets only in Server Components or API routes
Don't: Import secrets in client components

Description: Store local environment variables in .env.local
Do: Create .env.local for development secrets
Don't: Commit .env.local to version control

Description: Incremental Static Regeneration updates static pages
Do: Set revalidate period for pages
Don't: Rebuild entire site for content updates

Description: Pre-render dynamic routes at build time
Do: Export generateStaticParams for known params
Don't: Generate all dynamic pages on-demand
Code Good: export async function generateStaticParams() { return posts.map(p => ({ slug: p.slug })) }

Description: Control rendering strategy per route
Do: Set dynamic = force-static or force-dynamic as needed
Don't: Let all routes use default behavior

Description: Partial Prerendering combines static and dynamic rendering
Do: Enable experimental PPR in config
Don't: Choose between fully static or dynamic
Code Bad: // Entire page waits for slowest data

Description: Link enables client-side navigation without full reload
Do: Import Link from next/link
Don't: Use anchor tags for internal links

Description: Control prefetching for performance optimization
Do: Set prefetch={false} for rarely visited links
Don't: Let all links prefetch by default

Description: Prevent adding to browser history for certain navigations
Do: Add replace for redirects and tab switches
Don't: Always push to history

Description: Set essential configuration options
Do: Configure images redirects and headers
Don't: Use default config without customization

Guideline: Use TypeScript config for type safety
Description: next.config.ts provides autocomplete and validation
Do: Use next.config.ts instead of .js
Don't: Use JavaScript config without types
Code Good: import type { NextConfig } from 'next'; const config: NextConfig = {}

Description: Handle URL redirects at config level
Do: Set redirects in next.config.js
Don't: Handle redirects in middleware for simple cases
Code Good: async redirects() { return [{ source: '/old', destination: '/new', permanent: true }] }

Description: Vercel provides optimal Next.js hosting
Do: Deploy to Vercel for automatic optimization
Don't: Self-host without understanding requirements

Description: Set output mode for different hosting environments
Do: Set output: 'standalone' for Docker deployments
Don't: Use default output for containerized apps
Code Bad: // Default output in Docker - large image

Description: Content Security Policy prevents XSS attacks
Do: Configure CSP in middleware or headers config
Don't: Omit security headers entirely

Description: Generate nonces for secure inline script execution
Do: Generate nonce in middleware and pass to components
Don't: Allow unsafe-inline in CSP

Description: Never trust user input in Server Actions or API routes
Do: Use validation libraries like zod
Don't: Process user input directly
Code Bad: const name = formData.get('name'); db.query(name) // injection risk
